title: cerinthe glabra var. alpina
intro: Cerinthe glabra var. alpina, also known as Mountain Honeyflower or Blue Skyflower, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is a member of the borage family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues and relieving pain. The health benefits of Cerinthe glabra var. alpina include its ability to soothe coughs and colds, reduce inflammation and anxiety, and promote relaxation. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs like thyme and lemon balm, to maximize its medicinal benefits.
